column3: Good evening, As you know, I had a meeting with Ludwig van Beethoven about his last symphonie. I give you a summerize of the meeting. The good news is that the Vienna symphonic orchestra works the first and second movement of this musical piece. The musicians all appreciate this music and are looking forward to play the final part. The bad news is that the composer need more money to finish the symphonie because he decided to compose a vocal part for a choir and four singers. He forgets to keep us informed but he is ready to work around the clock to finish the project as soon as he'll get more money. I'm sorry to tell you this, but it's absolutely crucial to find a solution. The orchestra has to perform this symphonie next month, right. Could you call me please as soon as possible. Best,

column3: France is a beautiful contry, visited by millions of people each year. But if you are invited to a French person's home, do you know the correct etiquette? Here are solme tips: 1. DON'T be late. Make sure you arrive a few minutes early and wait. 2. DON'T sit too close to people. Some people stand so close that I can smell what they had for breakfast. You're expected to leave at least two feet between you and the person you're speaking with. 3. DON'T cross your arms across your chest, unless you're a king or queen. People might think you're angry or bored.  4. DON'T ask personal questions. If you ask questions that are considered rude  'How much money do you make?' 'How old are you?' 'Are you married?'  you might not get an honest answer.

column3: In my studies I used to make many presentations in english and spanish. I remember a presentation in english that I made with a colleague, this presentation was about rugby and british identity. I was in charge of the describing part of the differents rugby teams in Great Britain. I explained the history of each team, the symbols and the anthems. My colleague made the part that describes how rugby can reflect the british identity. Her part was more general than my mine. At the end we concluded that as French people we could believe that rugby has an important place in Great Britain but in reality football is most famous, in fact like in France. For this presentation we had good points, we made great slides with pictures and sound, we spoke clearly without reading our notes but we had also bad points, we didn't have time to review our presentation together and there was a bit too long for our teacher. To conclude this presentation was not perfect but I learnt a lot about rugby and it was the only grade for the course of British Civilization and I passed it.

In my country (Morocco), there is a law that imply that arab couples cannot be able to make a reservation in a hotel without the act of wedding. On the other hand, all western tourists can do it, and no matter if this tourist is with her girlfriend or a prostitute. One day, I was with my girlfriend on vacation and we were looking for a place where to sleep, it was very difficult because we are arabs and muslims. When you ask the hotel manager, he explain that the law is that way because of religious aspect : In Islam it's a sin to have a girlfriend and to sleep with her before getting married, so that's why we are not allowed to get a hotelroom reservation. Also, he said that we apply the law in each case, we are muslim, so we must respect the law of arab countries, and if you are european for example, then we will apply laws of your country and this in Morocco. They believe that if they apply islamic laws to the tourists, the economy will decrease swiftly. I can understand this point of view, wich is purely economic, but I think that we are a little bit hypocrite in Morocco, we have to whether seperate religion from the state or totally apply the islamic laws for every one.%%

column3: I've been working for 20 years. Therefore I can say that I'm now  able to easily identify office politics. Initially I was the one to pretend that office politics are not a game to be played. I was rather considering that our abilities are essential to get promoted. Now my opinion has been changed. I definitely consider that life at work is a game that everyone should play. Otherwise you will never be integrated into your company. Basicaly everyone should consider that there is time every day to talk with colleagues in order to improve our relationship. By doing this, we will be more appreciated by our colleagues who can advocate us once it's necessary. But for me it's an effort as it's not part of my personality. Like many people I tend to only talk to  persons I like working with. But it's a fact that everyone needs to be a " team player ". It's more an more one of the first crieria we take into account when we assess people ( yearly people review). I'd like to focus on one important point as well. Our respective bosses are all overloaded and once something's happening, negatively impacting results of our company for insteance, they like to consider comments brought by some people in the team.%% Therefore we need to have someone willing to advocate us when our name is put on the table.

The competition being hard , the customers requiring more and more the upper management of the most companies must take in consideration external and internal threaths. The main external threaths are competition and economical environment, the main internal threaths are : the lack of trust and motivation from the employees ; a bad organisation and communication within the company. The internal threaths can be real weaknesses for the company and affect the expansion, competitivity and profits. Nowadays some solutions are put in place to resolve this kind of weaknesses. Brainstorming and team building are the most current methods used to improve communication and trust inside of the company. Also it's really important for the managers to encourage the motivation and commitment of the employees. Involving people into a project it's a critical success factor and requires to lead interesting meetings, to discuss and listen ideas.  In conclusion any company must consider their employees like a first asset, must promote a corporate culture. The key of the success is the communication and consideration between individuals.

We are going to adopt in my country a very strange law relating to the right to abort. This right was granted to women in 1976 provided their pregnancy was less than 10 weeks . Nothing else was required and in particular the position that the father may have has not been taken into consideration. It is a right that women have Some associations since 1976 have raised relevant issues following the implementation of this law . Among others the fact that the number of abortions was supposed to decrease while it remains stable at 200 000 per year or the need to properly inform those who have decided to abort about all the aspects of an abortion. Now our new government wants to adopt a law that will forbid any kind of information which could be considered as 'against abortion' , typically some websites , family advisors or even priests . Apparently authorities are fearing a new position of a significant part of the population who consider that we have gone to far as basically we can do almost anything that we want with the 1976 law and its subsequent amendments. It looks pretty strange to face such a situation in a country that claims that it is the country of the human rights and freedom of expression !%%!

When I was young I loved horses, and I took the first opportunity for riding one. As a result of my passion, my father bought me a horse and I could move around our house in our beautiful the country when and where I wanted ! After many years, when I earned money enough, I went to stables for taking riding horse lessons with an instructor who was a former famous jumper. He allowed me to have a good level in show jumping. But I had also many possibilities to attend serious accidents, especially the death of a horse in a show jumping competition. This accident made me consider that this sport was dangerous also for horses. Afterwards I was afraid for my horse and gradually this anxiety hindered me to riding properly. At the end I stopped competition and show jumping. Now I do only dressage.

column3: Right now,  I'm in High School,thus, I don't have any job career. However, if we consider babysiting as a job then I've been doing that some months earlier. My plans for the future ? Well after High School I hope I'll be able to go to an American University so that subsequently I could work in America and perhaps I could be a movie translator ! Nevertheless, money is a big trouble as studying in the USA is really expensive, that's why prior to that I would like to go to England and work as an au pair girl so that I could take care of children, take English classes and be paid. While in England, I would keep money aside as much as I can and improve my English in order to be able to study overseas. It's only been a year or two since. I really wanted to study aboard, prior to that I wanted to study where I live, in France then get a licence in LEA which is about English and Spanish and afterwards I would work as a movie translator in France ir someplace else.

Since i was born i feel like things are getting worse and worse. The famous crisis is getting permanent. It seems to me that i've always known that global crisis. Unfortunately we are not concerned the same way. I can afford a car, a place to live and food for my whole family other do not have that chance. That world has that drawback, though it can be wonderful, nothing is fair, nothing is shared. Everyone could be happy and live well. But we all expect the best, without caring the price of that terrible wish... Let us be optimistic, and how about living in a better world, where money would not be a problem, for anyone !!

In France, since two years the bottom is dropping out of the national economy. A lot of people are loosing their jobs, taxes and prices are increasing too much. We can see that more more people are pessimistic and sometimes even depressive.  The national debt is increasing every year, our country is becoming impoverished and no serious plans are developped. The media are also responsible for this bad state of mind, they are always speaking about all that and I am sure that they could, from time to time, speak about positive things. That would help to keep a dynamic and positive attitude. So when you have a roof over your head and food on the table you can consider that all is fine for you. You can learn to be happy and enjoy being without too many problems.

The main purpose of this excerpt is to provide some information about how people should react when they face a career path confusion. The author uses an interesting way to explain that workers must focus on their feelings, hobbies or needs to determine which job could make them happier, which job could correspond the most with their personality. Even though it is likely not so difficult to find such a position, I guess the author forgot many important points, especially in the current crisis which has been affecting all the industries. Obviously, people must choose a position which will satisfy their needs, but according to their skills and experiences, not only feelings. When you apply for a position, the motivation is absolutely not enough for your employer, you have to have at your disposal a real background, the requested diplomas and sometimes must be strongly recommended by another people.

The last sales meeting where I attempted , was really clear about the target which must be achieved by the sales department next year. I heard with pleasure the presentation of our manager about the difficulties meet over this year and the solutions taken to keep the customers. Also the presence of the CEO to this sales meeting showed us that the sales department had a real strategic mission in our company and we had to carry on our effort. However I have some remarks about this sales meeting . Firstly if our manager had announced the key points at the opening of the meeting it would have been easier for us to be more attentive; secondly if each sales representative had presented their sales action over the year it would have been more constructive. Thirdly if our manager had let the sales representatives talk it would have allowed to the CEO to show their commitment. Except the below bad points , this sales meeting remain positive and  it will be usefull for the next year.

column3: When I was growing up, in the middle 1960s, internet didn't be found, regardless general public didn't use it in France. The only media we knew were books, newspapers and television; however, they were less widespread than today. It's difficult to imagine, but everybody didn't own a television. Subsequently, we really were less informed, and especially less quickly, about what happened in the world. Dictator's life was far easier... Internet was a groundbreaking invention, which changed the world as deeply as the industrial revolution did in the last 19th century. As for me, internet totally changed my way of learning. Nowadays, if I search for an information, at any time, about any topic, I take my laptop and I immediately access to any article, anywhere in the world. It's absolutely fantastic! In the past, I must go to the library and, with luck, I could consult a few books and nothing else. When I was a schoolboy, I learned English with a book; today, I curl up on my couch and I connect to Englistown. I study by watching video clips, and my private teacher lives in Capetown! The only thing that internet didn't change for me is my way of reading books: I continue to prefer paper.%%

The last couple of years was difficult for the country where I live. Because of the political turmoil and war the economy was down. Many people lost their job or had financial problems because they had to take charge of their extended family ,who was affected by the war. The market price was high and people struggle to have food on their table. Fortunately things are looking up now. There are more and more people who can pick up works. It still difficult for people to be thriftier because of the rising of market place. The government is managing to keep the inflation rate low. I think in the future things would turning around.

In France, when you are arriving into the restaurant you are expected to go first before women if you are a man, because all the people inside will like at you and not the woman. So, at the table, you have to let seat the woman first. It's bad manners to put elbows or hats on the table. Manners have to do with showing respect toward others.You are supposed to wait every seat before eating.What is proper is to not talk with a mouth full.You are expected to stand up if a woman was to get up to go to the restroom. After sitting down to eat, place your napkin on your lap.Remember,never use you cell phone at the dinner table.

I like my life. Philosophically, I think I am always at the place I must be at a given time : because what I am, what I do is linked directly with my previous thoughts, behaviors, and actions. Of course, like every human being, I often dream about having a better position, a bigger income so I could purchase a bigger house and car. However, in twenty years in the same company, I had five different positions and each one was better than the one before. I don't see why this evolution should be different in the future: I always try to do my best. Nevertheless, I have two challenges: to master my level of stress in keeping a very regular practice of sports and to have a more satisfacting social life, I mean, to bind ou rebind deeper links with my friends and maybe to found a family.

I allready did extreme sport when I was more young. I took a lot of risk on my skis. I remember when, with my two friends, I went to L'Alpe d'Huez for skiing. Dear Sir, I am writing regarding the overtime I am due . I had to work extra hours during two weekends and I object that you refuse to pay this overtime. My contract clearly states that I should be compensated for my weekend work. Furthermore, you argue that these extra hours weren't your fault, this argument can't be considered as nothing in my contract allows you to refuse to pay me for something the company is not responsible of.  Consequently I believe I am within my rights to claim being paid for the two weekends I had to work. Should you refuse to pay, I would consider the possibility of suing the company for damages. I hope there is an opportunity to settle out of court. Yours sincerely MAP

column3: Smoking can harm the health and the government tries to limit as much as possible its consumption. Every year, new laws are voted to discourage consumers to go on. First, in a financial point of view, taxes on packs of cigarettes are often increased : the rising percentage of the prices is significant, so a lot of smokers complain that smoking is becoming a luxury. Furthermore, one tries to disgust consumer from tobacco : some advertisings make them feel guilty, and the producers are obliged to display on the packs shocking images revealing tobacco disastrous effects on the body. At last, smoking has been forbidden in more and more public places (restaurants, coffees...). Some smokers consider that such legislation represent a blow to individual rights, but the others have the right not to breathe smoke. As a matter of fact, non-smokers must be protected from passive smoking, which can have bad consequences.

In this day and age, despite what sometimes people use to say (especially elderly people), the etiquette still exists. In France, like in other countries, when you are invited at someone's or somewhere, for dinner, or an other special occasion, there are some rules that you have to respect. First, don't be late, or too late: when you are invited at someone's place, the best is to arrived few minutes after the scheduled hour, but not more than fifteen minutes. If you are going to an event, like, let's say, in an Art Gallery, for some kind of event, just be at time, or five minutes late. Then, an other advice is to bring something, a little present, when you're invited for dinner somewhere (except if you're going to the restaurant). The most common things are a bouquet of flowers, or a bottle of wine, but other things are also appreciated, like some special food, to taste at the aperitif. Of course, in all cases, turn off your phone before going to your invitation, because it is ill-considered, and very rude. Then, when comes the time of start eating, wait for everybody to be served before starting, it's respectful.%% Well, now you're ready to spend a good time at your next invitation. Last step : book a flight to France to taste some nice food !

In France, it has been applied a law forbiding to smoke in public areas like restaurant , bar, train bus station , transports and school. This law has been put in place the first February 2007. We can consider this law has been well accepted by french people. Even if this hasn't dicreased the number of smokers, this law has had a real benefit for all people. No-smokers are not longer troubled by the cigaret's odors and now smokers couldn't consider now to smoke in a bar or in their office. In my view, this kind of law is really usefull for our society ,  when this law has been applied I was smoker and I've never considered this law like unacceptable but like a new way of life. From my point of view the persons who haven't yet accepted this law and carry-on to criticize are too addict to the the cigaret. Their drug is most important than their neighbours, childrens or friends' health.

I always wanted take up white-water rafting. I love the water sports. When I was young I begun sport with canoeing in calm waters, little by little I wanted more thrills, so I took up white-water kayaking. Next I practiced this sport in gorgeous place, with huge rivers, sometimes even the ocean. I really loved this sport. But one day, I saw people who did white-water rafting. It seemed to be awesome, and also very scared. I didn't understand why, but I felt afraid of this sport whereas I always practiced extremes sports, like hang gliding, snowboarding, windsurfing or also skydiving. One day I decided to face my fears of white-water rafting, so I enjoyed down the river. To finish, I want to tell that I'll never regret to face my fears, and I plan to face others fears. And I recommend to everyone to face your fears.

column3: During the latest 30 years of the XXth. century, many famous landmarks were built in Paris: Centre Georges Pompidou, Montparnasse Tower, Louvre Pyramid, and Bastille Opera. Some of them were very controversial. But one thing is certain, they all will left a long lasting footprint in the urban landscape. In my opinion, the Centre Georges Pompidou is the most obvious failure. Its groundbreaking architecture and its garish aggressive colors are unnecessarily provocative and upsetting. In opposite, the Louvre Pyramid is certainly an unquestionable achievement. This magnificent glass and steel structure in the center courtyard stands out against the ancient architecture of the Louvre palace. It has become the main entrance of the place. Each visitor can experience how this museum is modern and living and no more as asleep as it was before Pyramid's construction. To my taste, it's a masterpiece. Unfortunately, nothing that deserves to be mentioned has been built for ten years. The architectural policy is static since the mid 90's The Seine banks have been refurbished and cleared of cars. An important part of them are now a pedestrian area. But that's an improvement, not an ambitious urban policy. Without doubt, Paris needs a challenging architectural vision to be able to compete with other world renowned metropolises.%%

It's about time that people be conscious that the survival of mankind is linked to the way natural ressources are used. Green business provide to consumers products that are sustainable hence environmentally friendly. The best efforts have to be made among large corporations in the oil or wood industry which are constantly exloiting these ressources which we know are non-renewable . On one hand goverments encourage the green industry to develop, on the other hand they keep being dependant on fossil energy. Oil, Gas, Wood and Nuclear businesses are very lucrative. They generate a lot of jobs and revenues which are essential for a healthy economy. It's a challenging situation for decision makers I don't go out of my way to buy green products because I believe such a problem is a big issue that needs to be solved internationally and individually in people's heart and mind. I'm not quite sure that buying green products can help. It has mainly to do with men's behaviour. What is the real sense of wealth?

Paris is very well known for its variety of architecture. The haussmannian architecture is the most spread throughout the city and represents 60% of the buildings. The city owes a major deal of its beauty to Geoges Haussmann. One of the most touristic city in the world, the places that are the most attractive are buildings or monuments such as: The Eiffel tower, The Arc de Triomphe, The Pyramind du Louvre, The Georges Pompidou center. Among all of them, The best of the best is the Eiffel tower which is the most daring architecture with its 324 meters height. Beside this there is the business area of The Defense where are situated not only modern buildings but also the tallest in number. Each building differs from one another in shape and style but everything is well suited to give a splendid sight to the overall. At last, an important place is also given to parcs. That's why everyone can take pleasure to have a walk in the tuileries or the Luxembourg gardens.

France, is a country well-known for it's wonderful and mostly old-fashioned architecture. The towns of France make you travel to another time. There are a lot of Renaissance, gothic and Baroque architecture , particularly in the historical buildings such as St Peter's Basilica, Notre Dame or several castles throughout France. Paris, it's first city, is the perfect exemple of the melding of architectural styles, there are a lot of classical and antique buildings where people still live. However there are also a lot of modern and postmodern architecture, strangely enough it's not the youngest buildings but they really convey a feeling of innovation and modernism. The Louvre with it's glass and metal pyramid is really unusual and astonishing, people still cross the world to see it. And even if it was constructed in the 12th Century the use of metal was not the most beautiful material at this time. Finally, there is the Eiffel Tower, the tallest building in France with, 324m, entirely made of wrought iron and it's individual look. It's a really touristy building that I don't personally find beautiful during the day but at night, with it's thousands of lights it illuminates beautifully the streets of Paris.%%

Okay, I will tell you a story of a man who confirm that zombies do exist ! it tooks place in 1960's in the native village of my mother. One day, a farmer has died of his injuries after falling off a cliff, he was 45 years old. And naturally, we took her body to the cemetery and was then buried. Three days later, when her daughter and wife were having lunch, he opened the door of the house with eyes wide open and dust everywhere on clothing. His wife dies directly after a heart attack and his daughter became mad ! this man has won everything. In fact, at the time, there were no doctors in the village, so we decide to bury someone if we can not hear his heart beat anymore. But the truth is that the man had a severe bradycardia, that was all ! Anyway, the end of this story is dramatic, but admit that it's so crazy !

Provided that you have strong winds,wind power is quite effective. Building a small wind farm will provide our whole village with electricity. It's a cheaper way of generating energy. Why don't we built our houses with traditional energy-saving techniques. The way I look at it, we can do more. If each individual does their part, we can move mountains! In my opinion, we have to be active ourselves

In my city as I live in the small village, the only renewable energy that would be beneficial will be the solar power and wind power. We don't have an ocean or a strong river in my area, so it will not be possible to use hydroelectricity or tidal energy, even biomass can't be conceivable. Here, some homes have panels solar on their roofs and I have to say that this installation is really costly.  For promoting them to the future owners, it was said that they will save money if they purchase it. The first assumption of this operation was to obtain a tax reduction and the second was to sell the electricity provided by this concept to the government. In fact, the reality is that this investment is too costly and the return on investment is not profitable, because the government of course, dropped the cost of electricity in return. Concerning the wind power I heard that it is not safe for the health, independently the fact of the nuisance of the loudly sound. I think that everyone should take care of our environment by recyclable each thing possible, and try to save energy each time we can do it, because the future does not appear as happy days nowadays.%%

It could be great if we headlight the picture of the man who is totally attracted by this girl. The idea of Champagne is great to show how the party is lovely going and it demonstrates how people enjoy our products. If I speek about green business, for me it's the sale of products which are environment friendly. It also can be products which are produce in our area or country, not at the other end of the world. To be green I think the industrustry has to think about the packaging which often is too much for me. For example when I do my shopping , I always use a bag which I can be reutilised. I need my car to go to work every day but I try not to take it if not necessary. I also have a little garden where I plant  my own vegetables. I buy always tissurs which are recyclable. In my neighborhood many people have solar panel on their roofs; I taught to myself if I have to change my heating I'll also buy a green one which runs with air power and I'll put some solar panels on my roof too. I mean if a company built a new building, they also have to take care to utilise green material which are safer for our planet. My point is if everyone kate a little more care of what I buys and hopely what he puts in the rubish we can go ahead.%%

A lot of people would like to be successful but if so much people are writing about this topic, it is clearly because no one has found a recipe! It is true that one can observe some traits in a majority of successful people: be self-confident, motivated, creative and innovative... But it is not so simple. According to me, success doesn't only depends on you. The support of your family and your friends to believe in your capacities and to help you to realize your dreams is important. Then, if you are born in a wealthy or a successful family, it could be easier too. In this case, your family brings you the money you need to begin your project or can make you meet the people you need. Another point could be destiny and chance. Sometimes, you have the good idea at the good time or by chance you meet exactly the people you were needed to meet! This last point seems very important because in a lot of interviews of successful people they are talking about 'chance'... so, are you born on a lucky star?

column3: Green energy is one of the major issues of this decade, or even of this century. In fact, with the world population growing at such a high pace, energy needs are increasing exponentially. We're now trying to replace nuclear and coil power by wind and solar power to get to a more sustainable world. That's what we call "green business". My opinion about green energy is that it is necessary to start using them massively, in order to preserve the Earth for the future generations of humans and animals that will receive the legacy we will give them. However, hypocritical measures are taken by governments and companies to give the impression that they are acting in that way, while they are doing only the very requiered things. I'm not really going out of my way to buy green products, because they're often too expensive, but I try to act every day in order to reduce waste. For instance I reuse plastic bottles, I buy products in biggest quantities to avoid getting many little packages, and I recycle paper and glass.

I want to tell you about the the famous monnument of Paris : the Eiffel Tower !. The Eiffel Tower was built by the innovative great french architect Gustave Eiffel for "the global exhibit of Paris " in 1889. This tower is only built with steel and is height of 324 metres. This masterful tour have 3 levels using for sightseeing. It's not an apartement building. Nobody,  lives on the Eillel Tower.. You can only go to the restaurant or for admire the view around. The last and highest level is situed at 279 metres and forbitten for public. It' s reserved for scientific and weather exprients. An TV and radio antennas top the spire. This breathtaking tower is place along the river Seine in the center of Paris in an attractive ancien area the Seven arrondissement. When it first constructed this was the most tallest of the world and it lasts over 41 years in the past.  Now Eiffel Tower is not in the running of the most tallest tower but may be the most famous over the world. This monumment is begaming the symbole of the capital of the France. and the most visited in France.

column3: Uno is a card game. The rules are, every player starts with seven cards. The rest of the cards are placed in a pile face down. Next to the pile a space should be designated for a discard pile. The top card should be placed in the discard pile, the first player tries to match the card in the discard pile. You have to match either the number or color, for instance if the discard pile has a red card that is an 5 you have to place either a red card or a card with an 5 on it. If the player has no matches they must draw a card. You must lose all your cards, first player to do it is the winner.

STOP is a kind of word game. Although this game is for kids, the elderly also can fun, because it require speed to find words that start with a specific letter. At beginning, the first player start to say mentally the alphabet and when the time runs, the second player says STOP. The first have to say in which letter he stopped, so by turns, all players have to say words that begin with that letter. Sometimes, players are only allowed to use name of places. When the players can't think of a new word, the game is finished.

I really was so innocent, I was a young child. The magician was performing tricks of magic like, taking out doves from a hat, taking roses from I do not know where. I had never seen something like that in my life. I was really impressed and asking myself, Is it real? Is that possible?. Then, the magician also was a ventriloquy, you know, the man was speaking so that the voice seemed to come from a living rabbit. That was crazy and completely unbelievable for me at that age. I would define green business as the company that, in order to get economic growth, consider essential the social growth of its employees and users of its products or services, all from the sustainable use of the resources used: water, land, energy, etc... The first and last green company goal has a threefold aspect: creating economic value from social values and environmental. The green business, not only has to achieve the satisfaction of their partners or shareholders through customer satisfaction, selling more, but also it has to earn legitimately the satisfaction of every single part involved in the business activities, starting with its workers, following for its products or services and ending with the "satisfaction" of the environment it occupies, natural resources used and waste generated, care for the environment as his own. Finally, a good green company should try their best to communicate their good work and thereby help generate a sustainable consciousness that affects sustainable consumption. No, I don't go out of my way to buy green products, but I try to help the environment using the 3R's: Reduce, recycle and reuse.

Near, around 3 blocks to the south is Latin American tower, in the 90''s was one of the biggest buildings with 44 floors and all outside cover by glass. Now there are a corporate offices with buildings taller than Latin American tower, but this was one of the first and already survived to a major earthquakes, more than 8.5 grades. The last interesting place for me is the government palace, this is located in the downtown, has a really interesting architecture mixing many styles. Some of the areas are housing a collection about Mexico history. The only thing that you have to consider if you want to visit those places is the heavy traffic in the city.

Bank in Paris robbed by magicians in Las Vegas- A show hold in MGM Grand at Las Vegas was the place to steal a Bank in France. Las night show at the MGM Grand at Las Vegas, Nevada, presented how the Four Knights, magicians, stolen the Capital Bank in Paris, France. With more than five hundred persons in the show, the presenters showed all the people how they transport to Paris and draw the money out the bank. The magicians hypnotized a person from the public and sent him by teletransportation to Paris, he appeared just inside the vault, and he took the money and brought it back to the MGM Grand. But the robbery did not finish there, after the person came back through the teletransportation machine, it started raining money in the whole theater, so the audience could get some euros to take to home. FBI and Interpol are performing an investigation about the case when they found that the Capital Bank of Paris actually was stolen, so they are interviewing every one of the four magicians to solve this case and get the responsible for it. Reporting at Las Vegas, Nevada; Everardo Hernandez Sagarnaga.

column3: BRIEF DESCRIPTION TO CHOOSE THE MOST EFFICIENTLY KIND OF ENERGY MUST BE USED IN OUR CITY'S NEEDS. Insolation, 5.5kWh/m'/day, Wind speed, 4.3m/s month average. Obtained from L. Research Center Atmospheric Science; These comparative sheets show us the different meteorological measurement in Guadalajara, (the city where I live). Solar Energy and Wind speed. As you can see, Isolation (the solar irradiation) is an average of 5.5 kilowatts hour per square meter. Considering common houses has a television, a refrigerator, 8 light bulbs and some appliance like electrical oven, pc, wash machine and a water pump. It's consumes approximately 5 to 8 kWh each day. The electrical local company has a program who can buy you the electricity you don't use and exceeds what you need. Furthermore, during the day (morning and afternoon) where you dont use that energy, you can sale it to the electrical company, and during the night, where your needs are higher, you could buy it exactly what you need; it reduces your technical investment as is in saving the energy (batteries). In my opinion, solar energy will be the most effectively and environmentally friendly to solve the problem of energy in our city.%%

I live in Mexico, here the people with a bad behavior are punished! We have 2 kinds of jails, it depends of your age. If you're under 18 years old you can go to the correctional, I've heard that this kind of jail is less agressive than the adult's jail. Here we don''t have the death penalty, (electric chair,hang, lethal injection,...) no matter if the crime has been a serious one! We don''t have a court or a judge who can see the evidence neither. But we have the life impresionment !! In my particular point of view I don't think the prisoners can be reformed !!! For that I agree with the death penalty for mayor crimes: killers, violators, raptors, Why we should maintaining a person in jail who are not good to the society ?, I mean, someone who can give anything good for anyone?. Even we could be in risk, because this person could continuing commit a crime!!!. But unfortunately in my country the law hasn't been applied correctly, for that reason innocent people may be punished unfairly...
